theme: AI washing, false AI claims, and human-as-AI cases
goal: Recover exact caption and case-number metadata from the already captured DOJ-linked indictment PDF for the Nate matter without using credentialed court access or new harvesting.

priority_case.task: Run bounded OCR or image-to-text extraction on page 1 and, if needed, pages 1-2 of the locally captured indictment PDF to recover exact caption, case number, and filing-date block.
expected_outputs: Exact caption string as it appears on the indictment
Any visible filing-date or court-division metadata from the caption block
Confidence note distinguishing exact OCR from partial/ambiguous OCR
Machine-readable patch targets for shared ledgers if confidence is adequate

safe_resource_limits.network: none required after local inputs are present

paper_thesis_use: Promotes the Nate hidden-human row from summary-page dependence toward exact document normalization.
Supports a methods note separating compute-bound normalization blockers from source-discovery blockers.
Feeds reviewer-safe court-anchor and follow-up-barrier dashboard facets.
success_condition: At least one exact caption-level field becomes shareable with explicit confidence, or the OCR attempt yields a reproducible failure note that sharpens the blocker beyond the current image-only classification.
boundary_notes: No PACER login, fees, credentials, or new portal actions.
Do not convert charging-stage allegations into adjudicated facts.
